subject: Databases
title: Replication Lag Patterns
description: A short lesson to help you apply Replication Lag Patterns.

hook.text: A user updates their profile, refreshes the page, and sees the old data. Replication lag strikes again.

buildup.text: Read-after-write consistency: after writing, the user should always see their own update.

discovery.text: Fix: route the user's reads to the leader for a short time after their write. They always see fresh data.

twist.text: Monotonic reads prevent time travel: if a user reads version 5, they should never see version 4 next.

climax.text: Sticky sessions pin a user to one replica. They always read from the same follower, preventing inconsistency.

punchline.text: Lag happens. Route reads wisely to mask it from users.

quiz.question: How does read-after-write consistency work?
options: After writing, the user's reads are routed to the leader so they always see their own update
All reads are always sent to every replica simultaneously
The database delays all reads by a fixed amount of time
